channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: How the Reflecting Pool Turned Green: Missing ‘Bubblers’ and a Rush Job
item.description: Bulky “nanobubbler” machines were carted off ahead of a promotional event for President Trump’s Ultimate Fighting Championship birthday party.
item.dc:creator: Maxine Joselow and Luke Broadwater

item.title: The Big Wimbledon Preview
item.description: All eyes are on Serena Williams as she is set to play Maya Joint in the opening round.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Whitaker, David Law and Matt Roberts

item.title: Here’s What 250 Years of Fashion in America Looks Like
item.description: One way to understand the promise of this country is to look at what people have worn while chasing it.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: What to Know About Problems at the Reflecting Pool and Trump’s Claims of Vandalism
item.description: The administration has provided little evidence to back up some of his assertions about the Lincoln Memorial pool. Experts say other factors could have caused the major problems that have plagued it.
item.dc:creator: David A. Fahrenthold, Maxine Joselow and Clarence Williams

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A maintenance worker cleaned around the perimeter of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool while National Guard troops patrolled on Friday.

item.title: Pete Buttigieg Says He Was Separated From His Children in ‘Swatting’ Attack
item.description: The former transportation secretary recounted being kept away from his 4-year-old twins overnight after an anonymous report accused him of posing a threat to them. The police said the report was false.
item.dc:creator: Emily Davies

media:content.media:credit: Thalassa Raasch for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Pete Buttigieg, the former transportation secretary, is a prominent Democrat who is widely seen as a potential 2028 presidential candidate.

item.title: U.S. Forces Attack Iran
item.description: Also, a former Trump adviser pleads guilty. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times

item.title: Washington Theater Leader Is Out on Opening Night of TLC Musical
item.description: The departing artistic director, Hana Sharif, made it clear in a resignation email that she was leaving Arena Stage under pressure.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson

media:content.media:credit: Gabriella Demczuk for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Under Hana Sharif’s leadership, the nonprofit Arena Stage presented several musicals with commercial ambitions, including “Swept Away,” a revised “Damn Yankees” and “Chez Joey,” a reimagined take on “Pal Joey.”

item.title: As Foreign Aid Arrived, Many Venezuelans Asked: ‘Where is Our Military?’
item.description: The nation’s armed forces seemed to arrive late into the emergency response — with an emphasis on controlling traffic, even as bodies remained buried under rubble.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Members of the Bolivarian National Guard of Venezuela searching for survivors in Pinto Salinas, Venezuela, on Wednesday after two earthquakes hit the country earlier that afternoon.

item.title: ‘Pride Match’ Celebration Proves Awkward for Iran-Egypt Game in Seattle
item.description: Although homosexuality is illegal in both countries, their World Cup game has been designated a “Pride Match” by Seattle’s organizing committee to mark the month celebrating the L.G.B.T.Q. community.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A truck outside the stadium in Seattle on Friday delivered a message in protest of the upcoming “Pride Match.”

item.title: Tackling the Politics of the World Cup
item.description: The soccer tournament has brought a flood of political story lines.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei

item.title: A floating convenience store appears in Toronto
item.description: “Global Convenience,” an art installation honoring the city’s role as a World Cup host, is a near-perfect replica of a corner shop. Someone got stranded after swimming out to it last week.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald

media:content.media:credit: Trevor Wheatley, Cosmo Dean, and Puncture Design

item.title: Venezuelans in New York Confront Earthquake Back Home
item.description: The earthquake that leveled buildings and killed hundreds compounds years of grief for Venezuelans whose country has experienced political and economic collapse.
item.dc:creator: Christina Caron and William Neuman

media:content.media:description: Yibran Espinoza, 38, and Marian Galindez, 37, with their family and friends at El Budare Café, one of the Venezuelan restaurants that are accepting donations to send supplies to Venezuela.

item.title: Bill Pulte Picks G.O.P. Election Operative for Spy Agency Job
item.description: Christina Norton is the new chief of staff at the Office of the Director of National Intelligence, former officials said.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Dustin Volz
item.category: Office of the Director of National Intelligence

media:content.media:description: Bill Pulte became the acting head of the department of national intelligence last week after President Trump named him to replace Tulsi Gabbard.

item.title: In Venezuela’s Rubble, Listening for Whispers And Longing for Help
item.description: Facing criticism from residents that it was not doing enough to help, the Venezuelan government said it had dispatched more than 100 heavy machines to clear debris.
item.dc:creator: Fabiola Ferrero, Julie Turkewitz, Luis Ferré-Sadurní and Alan Yuhas
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Fabiola Ferrero for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A survivor on Friday. About 1,400 buildings had been damaged, including 13 hospitals and 25 shopping centers.

item.title: U.S. Military Strikes Missile and Drone Sites in Iran
item.description: U.S. Central Command said the attacks were in retaliation for an Iranian drone strike on a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday. They concluded after about 90 minutes, according to a U.S. official.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from Oman on Friday.

item.title: Man Pleads Guilty to Fraud in Sale of Art to Springsteen’s Manager
item.description: Thomas Doyle was accused of unlawfully pocketing most of the proceeds from the sale of a painting by Gustave Courbet to Bruce Springsteen’s manager.
item.dc:creator: Zachary Small

media:content.media:description: Gustave Courbet’s “Mother and Child on a Hammock,” which is dated to roughly 1848.

item.title: Migrant Camps Swell in South Africa Amid Growing Anti-Immigrant Threats
item.description: A threat to all undocumented foreigners to leave the country by Tuesday has led many to set up street camps as they try to get out.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon and Joao Silva

item.title: How the Supreme Court Removed Key Protections for Immigrants
item.description: Our Supreme Court reporter Ann E. Marimow describes how a 6-3 majority of the justices removed protections for immigrants and asylum seekers amid the Trump administration’s crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow, Gilad Thaler, Jon Miller, Nikolay Nikolov, Stephanie Swart, Rafaela Balster, Ray Whitehouse, Paul Abowd and Allison McCann

item.title: Saks Emerges From Bankruptcy With a New Plan and Corporate Name
item.description: The retailer, renamed Exemplar Luxury Group, is ditching anything that isn’t focused on high-end department store shopping.
item.dc:creator: Kim Bhasin

media:content.media:credit: Ahmed Gaber for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Geoffroy van Raemdonck, chief executive of Exemplar Luxury Group, the renamed parent company of Saks Fifth Avenue.

item.title: At the Great American State Fair, MAHA Meets Sno-Cones and Corn Dogs
item.description: Visitors can chow down on standard fair food while also attending health and wellness events.
item.dc:creator: Alice Callahan

item.title: Centrist Democrats Rebuke Party’s Left Wing: ‘We Are Capitalist, Not Socialist’
item.description: Following high-profile primary victories by democratic socialists, some moderate Democrats are advocating a different course, highlighting tensions in the party.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:description: A letter, titled “The Promise to America,” from moderate House Democrats, was led by Representative Tom Suozzi, a New York Democrat who flipped a swing district on Long Island two years ago.

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg Urges Meta to Explore Working With Polymarket and Kalshi
item.description: Mr. Zuckerberg’s plans for Arena, a prediction markets app that Meta is building, also include appealing to 18- to 34-year-old users.
item.dc:creator: Mike Isaac

media:content.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mark Zuckerberg, Meta’s chief executive, has made the development of a prediction markets app a top priority.

item.title: Debris Falls From the Sky After Plane Crash in Beijing
item.description: A lightweight aircraft registered to the airline Shuangyue General Aviation crashed into the Citic Tower in Beijing on Friday. The cause of the crash is unknown.
item.dc:creator: Meg Felling

media:content.media:description: The damaged exterior of Citic Tower, also known as China Zun, in Beijing, China on Friday.

item.title: 100 Reasons to Love Mel Brooks on His 100th Birthday
item.description: Here are 100 reasons to love the comedy writer, director and star who’s celebrating a milestone on Sunday and who’s changed our culture in surprising ways.
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis and Jason Zinoman

item.title: Why Lebanon Is Celebrating Brazil’s Win
item.description: Our correspondent Abdi Latif Dahir visits a Brazil national team World Cup watch party in Tripoli, Lebanon. Brazil is especially popular here as it’s home to the world’s largest Lebanese diaspora.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir, Christina Thornell and Coleman Lowndes

item.title: Olive Oil Cake
item.description: This simple, lemon-scented olive oil cake, recipe linked below, is an elegant treat all by itself or topped with whipped cream, fruit or ice cream.

media:content.media:credit: Romulo Yanes for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Vivian Lui.

item.title: Seared Zucchini and Halloumi in Red-Wine Vinaigrette
item.description: This spectacular but simple side dish, recipe linked below, pairs golden, crisp-tender rounds of zucchini with salty, molten cheese, a garlicky red-wine vinaigrette and fresh herbs.

item.title: Charred Corn and Chickpea Salad With Lime Crema
item.description: In the recipe linked below, charred corn is a bankable foundation for a simple and satiating summer meal. Just a few minutes in a hot pan will turn the kernels from starchy to sweet, while delivering smoky notes.

media:content.media:credit: Bryan Gardner for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Greg Lofts.

item.title: Why D.C. Locals Are Skeptical of Trump’s Championship Golf Course Plan
item.description: See the challenges with President Trump’s plans to transform East Potomac Golf Links into a professional tournament destination.
item.dc:creator: Joe Ward and Daniel Wood

item.title: Earthquake Tests Growing Ties Between U.S. and Venezuela
item.description: The Trump administration said it would commit aid, at a time when it has been expanding U.S. commercial interests in Venezuela beyond oil.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong and Michael Crowley

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Rescue workers and civilians looking for people trapped under debris in La Guaira, Venezuela, after two large earthquakes.

item.title: On the Streets of New York’s Little Haiti, Panic After Court Ruling
item.description: Residents in the Caribbean enclave in Brooklyn worried about returning to a crime-ravaged country after President Trump was allowed a path to deport them.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ley, Wesley Parnell and Miles G. Cohen

media:content.media:description: Brooklyn is home to about 65,000 people of Haitian descent. Nostrand Avenue, with its shops and restaurants, sits at the heart of the community.

item.title: Emily Blunt habla de su experiencia en “El día de la revelación”
item.description: La actriz creció viendo las películas del director e intentó “mantener la compostura” cuando él la invitó a una reunión sobre una película secreta.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Buchanan

media:content.media:credit: Thea Traff for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “I remember being completely traumatized by ‘Jaws’ and thrilled by ‘Indiana Jones,’” Emily Blunt said. “So it is a very surreal thing” when Steven Spielberg wants to work with you.

item.title: Ping-Pong’s Endless Summer at Rockaway Beach
item.description: Six artist-designed tables commissioned by Public Art Fund serve up serious fun in “Between Tides” at Beach 67. (Just take the A train.)
item.dc:creator: Hilarie M. Sheets and Jonah Rosenberg

media:content.media:credit: Jonah Rosenberg for The New York Times

item.title: Richard Scolyer, Cancer Expert Who ‘Became His Own Subject,’ Dies at 59
item.description: His lifesaving melanoma research in Australia illuminated the treatment he underwent for his own brain tumor, an ordeal he courageously shared with the public.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir

media:content.media:credit: Bianca De Marchi/AAP Image, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: Dr. Richard Scolyer in 2023. He became a high-profile public figure in Australia as he wrote and spoke about his experimental treatments for his own aggressive cancer.

item.title: Yves Lacoste, Who Exposed U.S. Bombing of Vietnam’s Waterways, Dies at 96
item.description: He used a geographer’s tools to demonstrate that North Vietnamese civilian infrastructure had been deliberately attacked, threatening millions of lives.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Yves Lacoste in 1992. The month after he had published his findings, the U.S. halted Operation Linebacker, a massive bombing campaign on the Red River Delta in North Vietnam.

item.title: Los terremotos en Venezuela azotan a un sistema de salud que ya estaba en crisis
item.description: Los bomberos están usando la luz de sus teléfonos porque hay escasez de linternas, y un hospital desbordado en la zona del desastre está funcionando sin agua corriente, dijo un médico.
item.dc:creator: Genevieve Glatsky

media:content.media:credit: Fabiola Ferrero para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Una mujer herida en los terremotos del miércoles fue subida a una furgoneta para ser trasladada el jueves desde un hospital de La Guaira, en Venezuela, a un hospital en Caracas, la capital.

item.title: D.C. Reaches Settlement in Lawsuit Brought by ‘Star Wars’ National Guard Protester
item.description: Sam O’Hara was playing “Imperial March,” the theme of the film series villains, while protesting the presence of National Guard troops in the capital when he was handcuffed by city police officers.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Members of the National Guard at Union Station in Washington last September.

item.title: Uber Enacts Stricter Background Checks for Drivers
item.description: The changes come after The New York Times revealed that the ride-hailing giant approved drivers with many types of criminal convictions, including violent felonies.
item.dc:creator: Emily Steel

media:content.media:credit: Joseph Seif for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Uber is expanding the list of criminal offenses that permanently disqualify a driver in the United States.

item.title: Yale Seeks Trump Administration Deal in Inquiry Over Race and Admissions
item.description: The university hired a high-powered law firm to try to reach an agreement with the Justice Department over claims its admissions practices hurt white and Asian applicants.
item.dc:creator: Michael S. Schmidt, Alan Blinder and Michael C. Bender

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Capozziello for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: While a deal could alleviate federal pressure on Yale, it could create problems closer to campus. Schools that have cut deals with the Trump administration have faced a backlash from students, faculty and alumni.

item.title: A Look Along Venezuela's Devastated Coast After Two Earthquakes
item.description: Buildings collapsed across the coastal region of La Guaira, north of Caracas, after Wednesday’s twin quakes, an analysis shows.
item.dc:creator: Leanne Abraham, Agnes Chang, Simon Ducroquet, Samuel Granados and Pablo Robles

item.title: Search Teams From California, Virginia and Florida Head to Venezuela
item.description: The groups that include firefighters, doctors and structural engineering specialists began arriving on Friday to join the race to find survivors.
item.dc:creator: Billy Witz and Shawn Hubler

media:content.media:description: Members of the County of Los Angeles search and rescue team on Thursday before leaving for Venezuela.

item.title: Virginia Man Sentenced to 40 Years for Setting City Councilman on Fire
item.description: Shotsie Buck-Hayes severely burned J. Lee Vogler, a councilman in Danville, Va., last year in an attack that the police said stemmed from a “personal matter.”
item.dc:creator: Christine Hauser

media:content.media:description: Shotsie Buck-Hayes during a preliminary hearing for his case in Danville, Va., in September.

item.title: Prosecutors in Washington Say Citations Have Been Issued at Reflecting Pool
item.description: A spokesman provided no further details, saying those who received the citations were ordered to appear in court at a later date. No records are available.
item.dc:creator: Clarence Williams

media:content.media:description: President Trump has blamed vandals for the myriad problems with the renovation of the reflecting pool in Washington.

item.title: Ann Blyth, Oscar-Nominated ‘Mildred Pierce’ Actress, Dies at 98
item.description: She was just 17 when she played Joan Crawford’s ungrateful daughter in the classic 1945 melodrama. It was the high point of a robust but short film career.
item.dc:creator: Anita Gates and Zachary Woolfe

item.title: Trump Threatens to Impose 100% Tariff on European Countries Over Tech Taxes
item.description: The president claimed the tariffs would override a trade deal with the European Union, which European officials finalized just days ago.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and Jeanna Smialek
item.category: Office of the United States Trade Representative

media:content.media:description: It’s unclear how soon President Trump could impose any of the new tariffs he threatened on Friday.

item.title: Flashback: Your Weekly History Quiz, June 27, 2026
item.description: Can you sort 8 historical events?

item.title: Gracie the Giraffe Is Found ‘Fat and Happy’ 2 Weeks After Escaping Texas Ranch
item.description: Gracie caused a stir after she slipped away into the Texas Hill Country, but was spotted during a helicopter search on Friday. Now it’s time to bring her home.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather

item.title: Gracie Abrams Understands the ‘Nepo Baby’ Criticism
item.description: Gracie Abrams talks with Joe Coscarelli and Jon Caramanica about fame, growing up in Hollywood and making her third album, “Daughter From Hell.”
item.dc:creator: Popcast

item.title: During World Cup Tour, Team Spain Feels at Home in Tennessee
item.description: A local printing business churned out hundreds of Spanish-language banners for a World Cup favorite now training in Chattanooga.
item.dc:creator: Scott Cacciola

media:content.media:credit: Julie Holder for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A recently installed billboard overlooks a field in Chattanooga where the Spain World Cup team has been practicing.

item.title: Venezuela busca a los sobrevivientes de los terremotos
item.description: El número de fallecidos ascendía a 920 y los hospitales estaban desbordados. Al menos 3300 personas resultaron heridas y cerca de 1400 edificios sufrieron daños, según las autoridades.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
